title: Reading to Apply a Diagram
instructions: Read the following email message about the diagram. Complete the email by filling in the blanks. Select the best choice for each blank from the drop-down menu.

sections.content:
Subject: Are you free for lunch?
To: Alina Lee <alinal22@xmail.ca>
From: Janet Foster <jfoster@xmail.ca>

Hey Alina,

How are you enjoying the __DROPDOWN_1__ so far? Listen, since you're going to be __DROPDOWN_2__ because of your presentation, I can order lunch for you and save you a seat at our table. Management wants us to meet up with our teams at lunch to discuss the morning workshops. I saw the __DROPDOWN_3__ when I passed the dining hall this morning. The selections sound pretty good. There's cream of asparagus soup or __DROPDOWN_4__ to start. For the main course, there's a wrap. Which would you prefer: vegetarian, chicken, beef, or pork? They all come with fresh veggies and __DROPDOWN_5__ with your choice of side. There are some really interesting desserts as well. I know sweets aren't your cup of tea, but they come with fresh berries and look delicious.

Let me know, and I'll see you at lunch,
Janet

sections.instruction_text: Using the drop-down menu, choose the best option.

questions.text: Janet and Alina know each other because
options: they met at the morning workshop.

questions.text: Janet and Alina are planning to eat lunch together to
When Janet says that sweets are not Alina's "cup of tea," she means that Alina
